facts.text: The covered bridge that gives Cornwall Bridge its name was built in 1864 and is one of only three surviving covered bridges in Connecticut.
Route 7 runs the length of the CDP, crossing the Housatonic River on the Cornwall Bridge in the northern part of the community.
Cornwall Bridge was incorporated in 1740, nearly four decades before the United States declared independence, and today includes Cornwall Bridge, Cornwall Village, and West Cornwall.
The Cornwall Historical Society, located at 7 Pine Street, is fully wheelchair accessible and will reopen its galleries on May 23, 2026.
Every June, the Cornwall Library hosts a two‑part weekend event called Books & Blooms, featuring a speaker and garden tours across the town.
